changes.to:
Frontend renders raw canonical message part JSON as plain text in chat bubbles at turn end.

## Observed
At the start of the assistant turn, raw JSON like this leaks into the chat UI:



This appears to be the raw canonical message parts array (thinking + tool_call) being displayed instead of being parsed and rendered properly by the frontend message renderer.

## Screenshots
- @uploads/IMG_8326.png — shows raw JSON at top of assistant response after user asks "What model are you ?"
- @uploads/IMG_8327.png — shows same raw JSON plus tool result sections below

## Expected
Thinking parts should render as expandable thinking blocks (or be hidden). Tool calls should render as compact tool-call pills/blocks. Raw JSON should never be visible to the user.

## Suspected area
Frontend message rendering pipeline — likely the message-to-React rendering path is falling through to a raw text display when it encounters parts it doesn't know how to render, or the message payload is being stringified and appended as text.

## Related
- oqto-dnj4 — model mismatch (same screenshots show both bugs)

changes.to:
Selected model on frontend is not respected by Pi - Pi starts with a different model than what the user selected.

## Fresh evidence (2026-05-10)
User has "MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.5" selected in the frontend model picker (bottom bar shows "fhgenie-preview" / "MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.5"), but when asked "What model are you ?", Pi reports it is running as gpt-5.3-codex (Azure Foundry) with Model ID Foundry-Codex/gpt-5.3-codex.

This is a live regression visible in production.

## Screenshots
- uploads/IMG_8326.png — model selector shows MiniMax-M2.5, user asks "What model are you ?"
- uploads/IMG_8327.png — Pi tool result replies "I am running as gpt-5.3-codex (Azure Foundry)"

## Suspected area
- Frontend model selection state not being included in the prompt command sent to runner
- Runner not passing model override to Pi startup
- Pi defaulting to a different model because OQTO_MODEL or equivalent env var is not set correctly

## Related
- oqto-xv6t — raw markdown leaking (same screenshots show both bugs)

changes.to:
Goal
Add a secure secret submission and use flow for RPC clients that routes secrets from client to owning runner and into runner-local kyz, without exposing secret values to agent/model transcripts, chat history, or tool logs.

Why
- Pi core cannot be changed; solution must be extension + Oqto platform.
- RPC clients need parity with TUI secure secret workflows.
- Future remote runners require locality: secret resolution/injection should happen on runner host.

Architecture
- Data path (secret bytes): Client -> Backend -> Runner -> kyz (local on runner host)
- Control path (metadata only): extension/agent commands and status events
- Secret values must never flow through chat message content, tool args shown to model, or persisted history.

Scope
1) Canonical RPC command additions (agent/files/ws channel as appropriate)
   - secret.submit (runner-routed)
   - secret.submit_status / command_response (metadata only)
   Payload (submit):
   - request_id (uuid)
   - session_id (platform session)
   - workspace_path (optional but preferred)
   - service, key
   - value (sensitive payload)
   - expires_at (short TTL)
   - origin metadata (client build/user id/device id)

2) Backend relay hardening
   - Route sensitive command only to owning runner of session/workspace.
   - Mark payload as sensitive in tracing pipeline:
     - no structured logs of value
     - no websocket debug echo of value
     - no persistence in hstry/oqto-log
   - Enforce max payload size and required fields.

3) Runner secure handling
   - Accept secret.submit only from authenticated backend channel.
   - Validate request_id TTL and replay window (request_id dedup cache).
   - Forward value to local kyz endpoint/CLI stdin path without shell interpolation.
   - Immediately zero/drop in-memory buffers post-use where practical.
   - Return success/failure + denial code (no value in error text).

4) Policy/authorization checks
   - Session/workspace ownership check (user cannot submit to foreign runner/session).
   - Optional allowlist for service/key namespaces per workspace/org policy.
   - Deny with explicit taxonomy: unauthorized, expired, replay, invalid_payload, out_of_scope, kyz_unavailable.

5) Audit events (non-sensitive)
   - Emit metadata-only audit records:
     - ts, user_id, session_id, runner_id, workspace, service/key, request_id, outcome, denial_code
   - Never include secret value or derived encodings.

6) Extension integration contract (pi-kyz)
   - RPC mode: /kyz-set must not solicit plaintext in chat.
   - Extension triggers secure client flow and awaits submit_status.
   - TUI mode can continue local masked input path.

7) Remote-runner readiness
   - Ensure backend always routes to runner-local kyz; no backend-global secret store fallback.
   - Define failure behavior when runner offline (queued? reject?). Prefer explicit reject with retry guidance.

Security requirements
- No value in logs (backend/runner/frontend), telemetry, traces, or persisted events.
- No value in command previews/tool output shown to LLM.
- No shell command interpolation containing secret value.
- Replay + expiry enforcement mandatory.
- Request correlation via request_id only.

Testing
A) Unit
- Payload redaction tests in backend logging path.
- Replay/expiry checks in runner.
- Ownership/authorization checks.

B) Integration
- End-to-end RPC submit success: secret appears in kyz on runner host.
- Verify no secret appears in:
  - browser console
  - backend logs
  - runner logs
  - hstry/oqto-log messages/events
- Denial-path tests: expired, replayed, unauthorized, malformed.

C) E2E (agent-browser)
- Trigger /kyz-set in RPC client flow.
- Complete secure form/input on client.
- Confirm agent receives only success message.
- Use secret via kyz exec/pipe path successfully.

Acceptance criteria
1. RPC client can set secret via client->runner->kyz with no transcript exposure.
2. Secret value is absent from all logs/history/events by automated checks.
3. Replay and expiry protections are enforced.
4. Ownership checks prevent cross-session/workspace misuse.
5. Works with remote runner topology (runner-local kyz only).

Out of scope
- Pi core changes.
- Full centralized secret broker replacing kyz.
- Bulk secret replication to runner hosts.

Refresh current directory on upload completion and auto-clear completed upload jobs. Fixes subdirectory view showing stale tree after upload and keeps upload row from lingering after successful write.

changes.to:
## Problem
Any sandboxed agent can read every session transcript across ALL workspaces (and other harnesses) regardless of profile. This violates the oqto-log access model ("scope derived from oqto-sandbox read permissions; no raw cross-workspace reads").

## Evidence (backend/crates/oqto-sandbox/src/config.rs)
- minimal: deny_read = ssh/gnupg/aws/systemctl only; ~/.pi unrestricted.
- development (default): ~/.pi, ~/.claude, ~/.local/share/claude, ~/.codex, ~/.local/share/codex all in allow_write (=> readable). lines ~466-475.
- strict: ~/.pi in allow_write (line ~548); deny_read covers ~/.config but not session dirs.
- spawn.rs: `_workspace: &Path` param is UNUSED (lines 26, 76) -> zero per-workspace session-dir scoping exists.

Net: an agent in workspace A can read `~/.pi/agent/sessions/--{other_workspace}--/*.jsonl` for every other project, plus Claude/Codex history. A duckdb one-liner slurps the entire corpus.

## Root cause
Harnesses must WRITE their own session files (Pi: ~/.pi/agent/sessions/--{safe_cwd}--/), so the home dir is granted writable+readable wholesale. No mechanism scopes reads to the current workspace's session subdir.

## Fix (dynamic, spawn-time; depends on cwd so not a static profile)
1. blanket deny_read on ~/.pi/agent/sessions (tmpfs mount).
2. rw-bind back ONLY the current workspace's `--{safe_cwd}--` subdir (Pi needs write).
   Mechanism already exists: strict re-binds ~/.config/oqto via extra_ro_bind AFTER the deny_read tmpfs (config.rs line ~561). Same layering.
3. Wire the unused `_workspace` param in spawn.rs to compute safe_cwd and inject the bind.
4. Generalize to ~/.claude and ~/.codex session/history dirs.

## Granularity
- Default: workspace-scoped (whole --safe_cwd-- subdir) -> maps to oqto-log Workspace scope.
- strict: session-file-only.

## Notes
- Found while building operator analytics CLI (~/scripts/bash/pi-sessions, trx oqto-qjcy). That tool is operator-only and intentionally NOT exposed to agents.
- Agent-facing session search is the correct path and already partly built (oqto-log FTS5 search_timeline); see epic oqto-2hyk.
- Add a regression test: agent in workspace A cannot read workspace B's session JSONL.

changes.to:
Implemented canonical session-ID persistence hardening for Pi JSONL imports to stop frontend history splits (messages present in JSONL but split across two oqto_log_sessions).

Root cause documented:
- duplicate oqto_log_sessions for same external_id (Pi session ID), e.g. one oqto-* platform session plus one legacy external-id session
- non-deterministic external_id lookup (`find_session_by_external ... LIMIT 1`) could pick the wrong row
- post-pass importer repair could write using mismatch session token directly, reintroducing split identity rows

Changes shipped:
1) backend/crates/oqto-history/src/oqto_log/ops.rs
   - `find_session_by_external` now deterministically picks canonical session:
     - prefer session_id==platform_id and `oqto-*`
     - then highest turn_count
     - then newest updated_at
   - added `list_sessions_by_external_in_workspace(...)`
   - added `delete_session_in_workspace(...)`

2) backend/crates/oqto/src/oqto_log/importer.rs
   - added `collapse_duplicate_external_sessions(...)` helper
   - after successful import/replace, collapse duplicate sessions for same external_id keeping canonical session only
   - fixed post-pass mismatch repair:
     - resolve mismatch token to canonical session/external IDs before replace
     - locate JSONL by resolved external ID
     - replace canonical session, then collapse duplicates

Validation:
- cargo fmt -p oqto -p oqto-history
- cargo check -p oqto-history -p oqto

Expected effect:
- no more split timelines where frontend shows only subset of messages while Pi JSONL has full history
- existing duplicates are automatically collapsed on subsequent import/repair passes

note: Canonicalized external->platform resolution and collapsed duplicate oqto-log sessions during import/repair to prevent frontend message loss.

changes.to:
## Goal

Migrate oqto from the legacy `mmry service run` / HTTP proxy integration to embedded `mmry-core` workspace-local memory files.

Target model:

```text
<workspace>/.mmry/mmry.jsonl
```

Use `mmry-core` directly from the oqto runner/user-plane. Do not depend on per-user mmry services, ports, TOML config, legacy stores, or `/v1/memories` proxying.

## mmry-core API

Use the renamed JSONL layer:

```rust
use mmry_core::{MemoryEntry, MemoryEvent, MemoryEventType, MemoryFile};
use mmry_core::agent_ctx::AgentCtx;
```

Open/init workspace memory:

```rust
let memory_file = MemoryFile::open_workspace(workspace_path);
memory_file.init(false)?;
```

Add memory:

```rust
let event = MemoryEvent::add(content, memory_type, tags, &AgentCtx::from_env());
memory_file.append(&event)?;
```

List memories:

```rust
let memories = memory_file.active_memories()?;
```

Search memories:

```rust
let hits = memory_file.search(query, limit)?;
```

Delete/remove memory:

```rust
let event = MemoryEvent::deprecate(memory_id, &AgentCtx::from_env());
memory_file.append(&event)?;
```

Deletion is append-only deprecation, not physical deletion.

## API mapping for existing frontend

Map `MemoryEntry` to the current frontend shape:

```text
id          <- memory_id
content     <- content
memory_type <- memory_type
tags        <- tags
created_at  <- created_at
updated_at  <- updated_at
metadata    <- metadata
category    <- metadata.category ?? "general"
importance  <- metadata.importance ?? 5
```

For update/edit, either:

```text
deprecate old memory + add replacement memory
```

or temporarily remove/disable edit support if simpler.

## Remove/replace oqto service integration

Replace dependencies on:

```text
mmry service run
~/.config/mmry/config.toml
per-user mmry ports
legacy stores
/v1/memories proxying
semantic/rerank memory service
```

Likely touched files:

```text
scripts/setup/18-services.sh
deploy/docker/entrypoint.sh
deploy/systemd/oqto-runner.service
deploy/systemd/mmry-embeddings.service
backend/crates/oqto/src/local/user_mmry.rs
backend/crates/oqto-host/src/linux_users.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/api/proxy/mmry.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/api/routes.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/user_plane/*
```

Replace `/api/workspace/memories*` with direct user-plane/runner calls into `mmry-core`.

## Behavior changes

Intentional short-term behavior:

```text
local lexical search only
workspace-local memories only
append-only JSONL source of truth
```

No short-term compatibility path for:

```text
semantic search
reranking
named stores
global stores
session dumps
service config
```

## Migration of existing memories

Use the mmry migration script, filtering out bloated hstry/session imports by default:

```bash
scripts/migrate_legacy_mmry_to_jsonl.py ~/.local/share/mmry/stores/mmry.db \
  --store oqto \
  -o ../oqto_refactor/.mmry/mmry.jsonl
```

Better long-term: migrate legacy store buckets into their corresponding repo workspaces, e.g. `oqto` memories into oqto workspace, `octo` memories into octo workspace, etc.

## Notes

This intentionally makes `mmry` the distilled durable-memory substrate, not a second session search/index. Session search and semantic/human search should live in separate tools/layers.

```text
tags        <- tags
created_at  <- created_at
updated_at  <- updated_at
metadata    <- metadata
category    <- metadata.category ?? "general"
importance  <- metadata.importance ?? 5
```

For update/edit, either:

```text
deprecate old memory + add replacement memory
```

or temporarily remove/disable edit support if simpler.

## Remove/replace oqto service integration

Replace dependencies on:

```text
mmry service run
~/.config/mmry/config.toml
per-user mmry ports
legacy stores
/v1/memories proxying
semantic/rerank memory service
```

Likely touched files:

```text
scripts/setup/18-services.sh
deploy/docker/entrypoint.sh
deploy/systemd/oqto-runner.service
deploy/systemd/mmry-embeddings.service
backend/crates/oqto/src/local/user_mmry.rs
backend/crates/oqto-host/src/linux_users.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/api/proxy/mmry.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/api/routes.rs
backend/crates/oqto/src/user_plane/*
```

Replace `/api/workspace/memories*` with direct user-plane/runner calls into `mmry-core`.

## Behavior changes

Intentional short-term behavior:

```text
local lexical search only
workspace-local memories only
append-only JSONL source of truth
```

No short-term compatibility path for:

```text
semantic search
reranking
named stores
global stores
session dumps
service config
```

## Migration of existing memories

Use the mmry migration script, filtering out bloated hstry/session imports by default:

```bash
scripts/migrate_legacy_mmry_to_jsonl.py ~/.local/share/mmry/stores/mmry.db \
  --store oqto \
  -o ../oqto_refactor/.mmry/mmry.jsonl
```

Better long-term: migrate legacy store buckets into their corresponding repo workspaces, e.g. `oqto` memories into oqto workspace, `octo` memories into octo workspace, etc.

## Notes

This intentionally makes `mmry` the distilled durable-memory substrate, not a second session search/index. Session search and semantic/human search should live in separate tools/layers.

changes.from:
Goal
Create one clear, auditable, low-drift deploy/update pipeline for Oqto by converging setup and deploy onto a single release layout and contract-driven activation flow.

Problem summary
- Two active deployment models exist today (transactional releases vs direct install/copy).
- Shipped vs editable files are not encoded in one authoritative manifest.
- Multi-user drift remains likely when files are copied ad hoc into user/system locations.
- Update behavior is split across setup update and deploy paths.

Outcome (definition of done)
1) One canonical runtime/install layout with explicit immutable/mutable layers.
2) One authoritative shipped-asset manifest in-repo, consumed by both setup and deploy.
3) Setup and deploy share a single activation/update mechanism.
4) Copy-vs-symlink policy enforced from manifest classes.
5) Workdir template and ~/.pi layering documented and implemented consistently.
6) Doctor/strict gates integrated pre/post activation.
7) Migration path for existing installs without downtime/data loss.

Scope
- Oqto repo deployment and setup architecture only.
- No behavioral changes to chat/session protocol beyond setup/deploy integration points.

Non-goals
- Rewriting all shell to Rust in one shot.
- Introducing a broad root daemon.

Acceptance evidence
- New docs + manifest committed.
- setup/deploy commands produce identical release activation semantics.
- E2E checks demonstrate no regressions for personal and team modes.

changes.from: Implement secure RPC secret submission path: client -> runner -> kyz (no agent transcript exposure)
changes.to: Secure secret submission: client -> runner -> SecretProvider (no agent transcript exposure)

changes.from:
Goal
Add a secure secret submission and use flow for RPC clients that routes secrets from client to owning runner and into runner-local kyz, without exposing secret values to agent/model transcripts, chat history, or tool logs.

Why
- Pi core cannot be changed; solution must be extension + Oqto platform.
- RPC clients need parity with TUI secure secret workflows.
- Future remote runners require locality: secret resolution/injection should happen on runner host.

Architecture
- Data path (secret bytes): Client -> Backend -> Runner -> kyz (local on runner host)
- Control path (metadata only): extension/agent commands and status events
- Secret values must never flow through chat message content, tool args shown to model, or persisted history.

Scope
1) Canonical RPC command additions (agent/files/ws channel as appropriate)
   - secret.submit (runner-routed)
   - secret.submit_status / command_response (metadata only)
   Payload (submit):
   - request_id (uuid)
   - session_id (platform session)
   - workspace_path (optional but preferred)
   - service, key
   - value (sensitive payload)
   - expires_at (short TTL)
   - origin metadata (client build/user id/device id)

2) Backend relay hardening
   - Route sensitive command only to owning runner of session/workspace.
   - Mark payload as sensitive in tracing pipeline:
     - no structured logs of value
     - no websocket debug echo of value
     - no persistence in hstry/oqto-log
   - Enforce max payload size and required fields.

3) Runner secure handling
   - Accept secret.submit only from authenticated backend channel.
   - Validate request_id TTL and replay window (request_id dedup cache).
   - Forward value to local kyz endpoint/CLI stdin path without shell interpolation.
   - Immediately zero/drop in-memory buffers post-use where practical.
   - Return success/failure + denial code (no value in error text).

4) Policy/authorization checks
   - Session/workspace ownership check (user cannot submit to foreign runner/session).
   - Optional allowlist for service/key namespaces per workspace/org policy.
   - Deny with explicit taxonomy: unauthorized, expired, replay, invalid_payload, out_of_scope, kyz_unavailable.

5) Audit events (non-sensitive)
   - Emit metadata-only audit records:
     - ts, user_id, session_id, runner_id, workspace, service/key, request_id, outcome, denial_code
   - Never include secret value or derived encodings.

6) Extension integration contract (pi-kyz)
   - RPC mode: /kyz-set must not solicit plaintext in chat.
   - Extension triggers secure client flow and awaits submit_status.
   - TUI mode can continue local masked input path.

7) Remote-runner readiness
   - Ensure backend always routes to runner-local kyz; no backend-global secret store fallback.
   - Define failure behavior when runner offline (queued? reject?). Prefer explicit reject with retry guidance.

Security requirements
- No value in logs (backend/runner/frontend), telemetry, traces, or persisted events.
- No value in command previews/tool output shown to LLM.
- No shell command interpolation containing secret value.
- Replay + expiry enforcement mandatory.
- Request correlation via request_id only.

Testing
A) Unit
- Payload redaction tests in backend logging path.
- Replay/expiry checks in runner.
- Ownership/authorization checks.

B) Integration
- End-to-end RPC submit success: secret appears in kyz on runner host.
- Verify no secret appears in:
  - browser console
  - backend logs
  - runner logs
  - hstry/oqto-log messages/events
- Denial-path tests: expired, replayed, unauthorized, malformed.

C) E2E (agent-browser)
- Trigger /kyz-set in RPC client flow.
- Complete secure form/input on client.
- Confirm agent receives only success message.
- Use secret via kyz exec/pipe path successfully.

Acceptance criteria
1. RPC client can set secret via client->runner->kyz with no transcript exposure.
2. Secret value is absent from all logs/history/events by automated checks.
3. Replay and expiry protections are enforced.
4. Ownership checks prevent cross-session/workspace misuse.
5. Works with remote runner topology (runner-local kyz only).

Out of scope
- Pi core changes.
- Full centralized secret broker replacing kyz.
- Bulk secret replication to runner hosts.
changes.to: Per ADR-0007. Path is client -> runner -> SecretProvider, where SecretProvider is pluggable (kyz/Infisical/Vault/env), not kyz-hardcoded. Secrets never enter the agent transcript or env in cleartext.

changes.from: NetworkMode::Proxy is config-only today; live posture is binary Open (full network) or Isolated (--unshare-net). Implement real egress capture for sandboxed agents: (1) confine agent in its own network namespace with a single host-managed veth; (2) host-side nftables DNAT all TCP to a transparent proxy (eavs in TPROXY/forward mode), force DNS to a controlled resolver, default-DROP all other traffic (UDP incl QUIC, ICMP, raw); (3) seccomp deny AF_PACKET/SOCK_RAW, no CAP_NET_ADMIN for agent. This is the enforcement half without which any credential-injection layer is bypassable via plain curl to arbitrary hosts. Note limits honestly: holds against misbehaving clients and in-sandbox processes, NOT against kernel LPE (that is the microvm tier). Design context: docs/active/design/20260609-isolation-tiers-and-egress.md
changes.to: Per ADR-0007. The egress PROXY already exists in eavs (eavs/src/egress.rs: transparent firewall, PROXY v2, SNI/Host domain ACL, DNS relay). The REMAINING work is the sandbox-side netns + DNAT redirect FRONT-END that captures agent traffic and feeds eavs over PROXY v2. Do not build a second proxy. Level-2 baseline; this unblocks truth-in-config on NetworkMode::Proxy (oqto-pgwp).
